definition: Assign RNA chains to sequence clusters and structurally dissimilar components, then compute binary or hierarchical group similarity. This audits whether structure-prediction performance depends on structural homology or near-redundant sequence families crossing train and evaluation sets.
example_usage: python tabular_group_similarity.py --train train_rna.csv --eval eval_rna.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --group-col structural_component

citations.title: RNA3DB: A structurally-dissimilar dataset split for training and benchmarking deep learning models for RNA structure prediction

evidence.summary: The paper argues that structurally dissimilar splits are needed to test generalization and avoid data leakage.
RNA3DB builds sequence clusters and structural components from sequence and structural homology.
The paper motivates component grouping to prevent leakage of structural information into the test set.

limitations: Binary group similarity is coarse and depends on the clustering thresholds.
Structural homology annotations may lag new RNA families or hybrid chains.
A continuous structural distance may be preferable when reliable alignments are available.
quality_gates: Check both sequence-cluster and structural-component overlap.
Treat chains that overlap multiple families or components explicitly.
